outputs.loading_convention: Vanilla <script> tag (after rules-engine.js) exposes window.AIOpponent. CommonJS-friendly for tests; the require() path resolves rules-engine relative to the builder directory layout so node tests work.

loading_order: rules-engine.js MUST be loaded before ai-opponent.js (see controller-and-shell index.html ordering).

contract_compliance.ai-opponent--controller-and-shell.json: matches: chooseMove(state, aiSymbol) -> number; never mutates input state (constructs hypotheticals).
contract_compliance.rules-engine--ai-opponent.json: consumes: legalMoves, applyMove, checkOutcome; does not duplicate win-line logic.

tie_break: deterministic first-encountered in ascending index order
